page: Amazon Web Services c8id.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) operating at 3.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities across compute and memory operations. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to a Geekbench single-core score of 2200.0 and a multi-core score of 19875.0. Memory bandwidth is notable, reaching 3859.38 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 194.95 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The instance also demonstrates high throughput in database and web serving tests, achieving 86.36 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 77.8 GB/sec in static web serving.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8id.12xlarge offers high resource density for compute-heavy workloads that benefit from fast local NVMe storage, though the 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications. The inclusion of AMX instructions enables efficient local machine learning tasks, as shown by the LLM inference speed of 330.79 tokens per second for text generation with a small model. This instance is suited for high-performance computing, database operations, web serving, software compilation, and localized machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 96.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, enabling strong performance in compute-intensive tasks. Benchmarks show a Geekbench single-core score of 2200.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 86.36 million operations per second. With a 2.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio, this instance is optimized for high-performance computing, database operations, and web serving, offering a balanced tradeoff between raw compute power and fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 2850 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong performance for databases and web serving.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring high-speed DDR5 memory, integrated local NVMe storage, and advanced matrix acceleration for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
96.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and AMX for matrix operations
Suited for high-performance computing, database operations, and web serving
